Plaintiff/Judgment Creditor Isec, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Nunc Pro Tunc Order Regarding Renewal Of Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Matthew R. Seifen, Scarleth Castrejon, Salvador Bustos, Martin Horwitz, And Jeff Oberlink In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Plaintiff/Judgment Creditor ISEC, Inc.'s Motion For Nunc Pro Tunc Order Regarding Renewal Of Judgment.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff/judgment creditor ISEC, Inc.'s motion for nunc pro tunc order regarding renewal of judgment is granted.

On March 23, 2010, the Court entered judgment by default in this action in favor of ISEC in the total amount of $80,373.35. ISEC shows that on March 19, 2020, it timely attempted to electronically file its application for renewal of judgment, but was unable to do so until the case had been updated for e-filing. It left copies of the papers in the court's drop box on March 19, 2020. When the papers were later resubmitted for filing, the clerk declined to file them because the ten-year period to renew the judgment had expired on March 23, 2020. ISEC's counsel did not receive notice of that rejection until June 15, 2020, and its subsequent efforts to resolve the issue with the clerk's office were unsuccessful.

Generally, a money judgment is enforceable for 10 years after entry, but the enforcement period may be extended by renewal of the judgment. (Code Civ. Proc. secs. 683.020, 683.120(a).) The application must be filed "at any time before the expiration of the 10-year period of enforceability provided by Section 683.020." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 683.130(a).) A court has the inherent power to enter a nunc pro tunc order "'to show that a thing was done at one time which ought to have been shown at that time. It is an entry now for something previously done so that the record may actually speak the truth.'" (Martin v. Martin (1970) 2 Cal.3d 752, 760-761.) Given the moving party's timely attempts to file its application for renewal of judgment, and its inability to do so through no fault of its own due to the unavailability of the Court's e-filing system and the delays occasioned by the curtailment of operations caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, entry of such an order is appropriate here. The Clerk is instructed to file the application for renewal of judgment and accompanying papers nunc pro tunc as of March 19, 2020.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Thomas Hesketh, Esq. appeared for plaintiff ISEC, Inc., a Colorado Corporation.
No appearance by defendant Christopher John Augusto, individually and doing business as Christopher J. Augusto Plumbing Company.
Default was entered against defendant on April 0, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed October 30, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff ISEC, Inc. a Colorado corporation, and against the Defaulted Defendant Christopher John Augusto, individually and doing business as Christopher J. Augusto Plumbing Company as follows:

Damages/Principal - $61,123.10.
Prejudgment Interest - Waived.
Attorney's Fees - $18,385.50.
Costs - $864.75, for a total judgment in the amount of $80,373.35.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
